Mtn To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Apr-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DANIEL P. MCINTOSH, AKA DANIEL P. MCINTOSHDAN, Motion to set aside Default and Default Judgment. Appearances: Daniel McIntosh (916) 652-7315 Defendant and Moving party appearing in Pro Per. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. Defendant must file his answer by 4:00 pm April 22, 2010. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Katy Anne Bersamin, CSR # 11722

Notice And Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.'S Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $340, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Notice And Motion For Appointment Of Receiver And To Appoint Craig A. Block As Receiver
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling adopted. Plaintiff Creditors Trade association, Inc.'s motion for appointment of receiver, and to appoint Richard B. Cole as receiver is granted. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
